mappings.rationale: Covers the broader context of Central African history including the Bantu-speaking peoples and the Kongo, Luba, and Lunda kingdoms.
Relevant to subsection B2 on the development of Bantu states and their political structures from c. 1400.
Relevant to the religious and cultural traditions of the Bantu-speaking peoples discussed in subsection B.
Relevant to subsection B1 on the Stone Age origins of Central African cultures and archaeological evidence.
Relevant to subsection B1 on the origins of Central African cultures in the Stone Age and the emergence of Bantu-speaking peoples.
Relevant to the broader patterns of state formation in Central Africa including the Kongo, Luba, and Lunda kingdoms.
